rationale: Gov't Code §1431.015 governs emergency anticipation notes for Gulf Coast issuers after hurricanes. It has no relation to Tax Code §11.13(b) homestead exemption amounts. No conflict exists.
quote: Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter or a rating requirement prescribed by Chapter 1371, an issuer located within 70 miles of the Gulf of Mexico or of a bay or inlet of the gulf may authorize the issuance of an anticipation note or other obligation in the event of an emergency.
_note: Rule text concerns emergency debt issuance; proposed homestead exemption increase is unrelated.
